name: Wild Salmon Crepes
recipeIngredient: 75 g Frozen chopped spinach (thawed and drained)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the can of salmon, reserving the juice. Break the fish into large pieces and set aside.
2. Mix together the thawed spinach and 75g (3oz) of flour. Beat the egg and milk, then gradually add to the flour mixture. Remove lumps by stirring well. Season with salt.
3. Heat a drop of oil in a crepe or frying pan. Pour 2-3 tablespoons of batter into the pan and rotate gently to spread evenly. Cook over moderate heat until the pancake begins to lift. Turn over and cook the other side. Repeat until all batter is used. Keep crepes warm.
4. Melt butter in a saucepan, stir in flour, then gradually add the reserved salmon juice and milk. Stir briskly to prevent lumps.
5. Add the salmon pieces, mushrooms, seasoning, yogurt into the sauce. Heat through. Place the salmon filling on each crepe and fold into triangles. Arrange on a serving plate. Keep warm.
6. Heat walnut oil in a pan and quickly fry the remaining mushrooms. Garnish the pancakes with mushrooms, lettuce, and spinach leaves.
Serves 2. Makes 4-6 crepes
Approx. 640 kcals per serving
